speaker: EDMUND
text: Save thee, Curan.

speaker: CURAN
text: And you, sir. I have been with your father and

speaker: CURAN
text: given him notice that the Duke of Cornwall and

speaker: CURAN
text: Regan his duchess will be here with him this night.

speaker: EDMUND
text: How comes that?

speaker: CURAN
text: Nay, I know not. You have heard of the news

speaker: CURAN
text: abroad?—I mean the whispered ones, for they are

speaker: CURAN
text: yet but ear-kissing arguments.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Not I. Pray you, what are they?

speaker: CURAN
text: Have you heard of no likely wars toward ’twixt

speaker: CURAN
text: the dukes of Cornwall and Albany?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Not a word.

speaker: CURAN
text: You may do, then, in time. Fare you well, sir.

speaker: EDMUND
text: The Duke be here tonight? The better, best.

speaker: EDMUND
text: This weaves itself perforce into my business.

speaker: EDMUND
text: My father hath set guard to take my brother,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And I have one thing of a queasy question

speaker: EDMUND
text: Which I must act. Briefness and fortune work!—

speaker: EDMUND
text: Brother, a word. Descend. Brother, I say!

speaker: EDMUND
text: My father watches. O sir, fly this place!

speaker: EDMUND
text: Intelligence is given where you are hid.

speaker: EDMUND
text: You have now the good advantage of the night.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Have you not spoken ’gainst the Duke of Cornwall?

speaker: EDMUND
text: He’s coming hither, now, i’ th’ night, i’ th’ haste,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And Regan with him. Have you nothing said

speaker: EDMUND
text: Upon his party ’gainst the Duke of Albany?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Advise yourself.

speaker: EDGAR
text: I am sure on ’t, not a word.

speaker: EDMUND
text: I hear my father coming. Pardon me.

speaker: EDMUND
text: In cunning I must draw my sword upon you.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Draw. Seem to defend yourself. Now, quit you well.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Yield! Come before my father! Light, hoa, here!

speaker: EDMUND
text:
Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Edgar
                .
                 
              Fly, brother.—Torches, torches!—So, farewell.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Some blood drawn on me would beget opinion

speaker: EDMUND
text: Of my more fierce endeavor. I have seen drunkards

speaker: EDMUND
text: Do more than this in sport.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Father, father!

speaker: EDMUND
text: Stop, stop! No help?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now, Edmund, where’s the villain?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Here stood he in the dark, his sharp sword out,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Mumbling of wicked charms, conjuring the moon

speaker: EDMUND
text: To stand auspicious mistress.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But where is he?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Look, sir, I bleed.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Where is the villain, Edmund?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Fled this way, sir, when by no means he could—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
Pursue him, ho! Go after.
                 
                Servants
                 
                exit
                .
               By no means what?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Persuade me to the murder of your Lordship,

speaker: EDMUND
text: But that I told him the revenging gods

speaker: EDMUND
text: ’Gainst parricides did all the thunder bend,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Spoke with how manifold and strong a bond

speaker: EDMUND
text: The child was bound to th’ father—sir, in fine,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Seeing how loathly opposite I stood

speaker: EDMUND
text: To his unnatural purpose, in fell motion

speaker: EDMUND
text: With his preparèd sword he charges home

speaker: EDMUND
text: My unprovided body, lanced mine arm;

speaker: EDMUND
text: And when he saw my best alarumed spirits,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Bold in the quarrel’s right, roused to th’ encounter,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Or whether ghasted by the noise I made,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Full suddenly he fled.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let him fly far!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Not in this land shall he remain uncaught,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And found—dispatch. The noble duke my master,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My worthy arch and patron, comes tonight.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: By his authority I will proclaim it

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That he which finds him shall deserve our thanks,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Bringing the murderous coward to the stake;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He that conceals him, death.

speaker: EDMUND
text: When I dissuaded him from his intent

speaker: EDMUND
text: And found him pight to do it, with curst speech

speaker: EDMUND
text: I threatened to discover him. He replied

speaker: EDMUND
text: “Thou unpossessing bastard, dost thou think

speaker: EDMUND
text: If I would stand against thee, would the reposal

speaker: EDMUND
text: Of any trust, virtue, or worth in thee

speaker: EDMUND
text: Make thy words faithed? No. What I should deny—

speaker: EDMUND
text: As this I would, though thou didst produce

speaker: EDMUND
text: My very character—I’d turn it all

speaker: EDMUND
text: To thy suggestion, plot, and damnèd practice.

speaker: EDMUND
text: And thou must make a dullard of the world

speaker: EDMUND
text: If they not thought the profits of my death

speaker: EDMUND
text: Were very pregnant and potential spurs

speaker: EDMUND
text: To make thee seek it.”

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O strange and fastened villain!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Would he deny his letter, said he?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I never got him.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Hark, the Duke’s trumpets. I know not why he comes.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: All ports I’ll bar. The villain shall not ’scape.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The Duke must grant me that. Besides, his picture

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I will send far and near, that all the kingdom

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: May have due note of him. And of my land,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Loyal and natural boy, I’ll work the means

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To make thee capable.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: How now, my noble friend? Since I came hither,

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Which I can call but now, I have heard strange news.

speaker: REGAN
text: If it be true, all vengeance comes too short

speaker: REGAN
text: Which can pursue th’ offender. How dost, my lord?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O madam, my old heart is cracked; it’s cracked.

speaker: REGAN
text: What, did my father’s godson seek your life?

speaker: REGAN
text: He whom my father named, your Edgar?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O lady, lady, shame would have it hid!

speaker: REGAN
text: Was he not companion with the riotous knights

speaker: REGAN
text: That tended upon my father?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I know not, madam. ’Tis too bad, too bad.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Yes, madam, he was of that consort.

speaker: REGAN
text: No marvel, then, though he were ill affected.

speaker: REGAN
text: ’Tis they have put him on the old man’s death,

speaker: REGAN
text: To have th’ expense and waste of his revenues.

speaker: REGAN
text: I have this present evening from my sister

speaker: REGAN
text: Been well informed of them, and with such cautions

speaker: REGAN
text: That if they come to sojourn at my house

speaker: REGAN
text: I’ll not be there.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Nor I, assure thee, Regan.—

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Edmund, I hear that you have shown your father

speaker: CORNWALL
text: A childlike office.

speaker: EDMUND
text: It was my duty, sir.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He did bewray his practice, and received

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This hurt you see striving to apprehend him.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Is he pursued?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: If he be taken, he shall never more

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Be feared of doing harm. Make your own purpose,

speaker: CORNWALL
text: How in my strength you please.—For you, Edmund,

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Whose virtue and obedience doth this instant

speaker: CORNWALL
text: So much commend itself, you shall be ours.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Natures of such deep trust we shall much need.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: You we first seize on.

speaker: EDMUND
text: I shall serve you, sir,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Truly, however else.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For him I thank your Grace.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: You know not why we came to visit you—

speaker: REGAN
text: Thus out of season, threading dark-eyed night.

speaker: REGAN
text: Occasions, noble Gloucester, of some poise,

speaker: REGAN
text: Wherein we must have use of your advice.

speaker: REGAN
text: Our father he hath writ, so hath our sister,

speaker: REGAN
text: Of differences, which I best thought it fit

speaker: REGAN
text: To answer from our home. The several messengers

speaker: REGAN
text: From hence attend dispatch. Our good old friend,

speaker: REGAN
text: Lay comforts to your bosom and bestow

speaker: REGAN
text: Your needful counsel to our businesses,

speaker: REGAN
text: Which craves the instant use.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I serve you, madam.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Your Graces are right welcome.
